title: T-006 — Reference.RolePermissions schema + Apex seed (NPD perms)
prompt:
# T-006 — Reference.RolePermissions schema + Apex seed

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §2.2 + N-U6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create Reference.RolePermissions schema-driven RBAC table and seed Apex base roles with the 19 permissions listed in §2.2 (including risk.write, compliance_doc.write, formulation.create_draft/lock, recipe.submit_for_trial, pilot.promote_to_bom, npd.gate.advance/approve).

## Implementation contract
Migration 0015 emits Reference.RolePermissions(role TEXT, permission TEXT, allowed BOOLEAN, scope_qualifier TEXT, org_id UUID, PK(org_id,role,permission,scope_qualifier)) + ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with policy `role_permissions_org_context` using `org_id = app.current_org_id()` (foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC). Seed inserts 6 roles × 19 perms = 114 rows for the Apex org per §2.2 matrix. Drizzle schema. Helper hasPermission(role, perm, scope?) returning boolean.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0015_role_permissions.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/role-permissions.ts [create]
- packages/db/seeds/role-permissions-apex.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/has-permission.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/__tests__/has-permission.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given seed ran, when querying for npd_manager + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=true; for core_user + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=false
2. Given seed ran, when counting permissions for admin role, then all 19 perms present with allowed=true except where matrix says ❌
3. Given hasPermission('technical_manager','compliance_doc.write','dept=technical') called, then returns true
4. Given pg_policies is inspected, when the role_permissions policy is listed, then the qual/withcheck references `app.current_org_id()` and contains no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC

## Test strategy
- RED: 6 unit tests covering matrix corners (npd_manager d365 yes; core_user d365 no; admin schema.edit yes; viewer fa.create no; technical_manager compliance_doc.write yes; npd_manager risk.write yes) — tests connect as app_user and call `app.set_org_context(session_token, apex_org_id)` before SELECT.
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db seed:role-permissions-apex && pnpm --filter rbac test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `tenant_id` as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is `org_id`.
- Do not bypass org scoping in queries
- Do not hardcode role names in production code (use seed config)
- Do not seed without scope_qualifier where matrix says 'own dept'

pipeline_inputs.description: Create Reference.RolePermissions table (org_id-scoped) + Apex seed of 19 NPD perms × 6 roles per §2.2 matrix; RLS uses foundation app.current_org_id().
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0015 emits the table with composite PK on (org_id, role, permission, scope_qualifier) + ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY. Policy `role_permissions_org_context` uses `org_id = app.current_org_id()` for USING/WITH CHECK. Seed file populates the Apex org with the full matrix. hasPermission helper queries by role+perm+scope. Tests cover 6 representative matrix cells; tests connect as app_user and call `app.set_org_context(...)` before assertions. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context).
out_of_scope: Do not implement MFA flow here (T-022 covers MFA gate for d365_builder.execute)
Do not build admin UI for editing perms
acceptance_criteria: Given seed ran, when querying for npd_manager + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=true; for core_user + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=false
Given seed ran, when counting permissions for admin role, then all 19 perms present with allowed=true except matrix-specified denials
Given hasPermission('technical_manager','compliance_doc.write','dept=technical') called, then it returns true

Given pg_policies is inspected, when the role_permissions policy is listed, then the qual/withcheck references app.current_org_id() and contains no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC
test_strategy: RED: 6 unit tests covering matrix corners before seed exists; tests connect as app_user and call app.set_org_context(session_token, apex_org_id) before SELECT
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db seed:role-permissions-apex && pnpm --filter rbac test'
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id.
Do not bypass org scoping in queries
Do not hardcode role names in production code paths
Do not omit scope_qualifier on dept-scoped perms
